title: E2E test script: full booking flow PWA → API → admin parity
description:
# Context

A self-contained Python script that exercises the full happy-path flow against a running uvicorn instance: fetch bootstrap, validate promo, submit booking, fetch bookings via token, verify admin shows it with the right badge. Lives at `tests/e2e/test_full_booking_flow.py` and is runnable both via pytest AND as a standalone `python tests/e2e/test_full_booking_flow.py --base-url http://localhost:8000 --admin-password ...` script for ops smoke tests.

# Why a separate e2e script (not just integration tests)

- Integration tests use TestClient — bypass HTTP, ASGI handles requests in-process.
- E2e exercises the real HTTP layer: CORS preflight actually fires, real timeouts apply, real BackgroundTasks scheduling, real DB commits.
- Same script runnable in staging/production smoke testing.

# Script structure

```python
\"\"\"E2E: PWA booking flow vs admin parity.

Usage:
  python tests/e2e/test_full_booking_flow.py --base-url <api_url> --admin-password <password>

Run as pytest:
  pytest tests/e2e/test_full_booking_flow.py
\"\"\"

import argparse
import logging
import uuid
import sys
import requests

LOG = logging.getLogger("e2e.full_booking_flow")

def run(base_url: str, admin_password: str) -> bool:
    LOG.info("=== STEP 1: GET /api/v1/bootstrap ===")
    resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bootstrap", params={"category": "A"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"bootstrap failed: {resp.status_code} {resp.text}"
    bootstrap = resp.json()
    assert bootstrap["categories"], "no categories"
    LOG.info("bootstrap ok: %d categories, %d wash, %d detailing", len(bootstrap["categories"]), len(bootstrap["services"]["wash"]), len(bootstrap["services"]["detailing"]))

    LOG.info("=== STEP 2: validate promo YS26 ===")
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/promos/validate", json={"code": "YS26", "category": "A"})
    promo = resp.json()
    LOG.info("promo valid=%s label=%s discount_count=%d", promo["valid"], promo.get("label"), len(promo.get("discounted_prices", {})))

    LOG.info("=== STEP 3: POST /api/v1/bookings ===")
    crid = str(uuid.uuid4())
    test_phone = "+212 6 11 20 45 02"  # canonical "212611204502"
    payload = {
        "phone": test_phone,
        "name": "E2E TestUser",
        "category": "A",
        "vehicle": {"make": "Clio", "color": "Bleu"},
        "location": {"kind": "home", "pin_address": "Test Anfa"},
        "service_id": "svc_cpl",
        "date": "2026-12-01",  # far future
        "slot": "slot_10_12",
        "addon_ids": ["svc_cuir"],
        "client_request_id": crid,
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"create failed: {resp.status_code} {resp.text}"
    booking = resp.json()
    LOG.info("booking created: ref=%s total=%d token_len=%d", booking["ref"], booking["total_dh"], len(booking["bookings_token"]))
    assert booking["ref"].startswith("EW-"), f"unexpected ref format: {booking['ref']}"
    token = booking["bookings_token"]

    LOG.info("=== STEP 4: GET /api/v1/bookings with token ===")
    resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": token})
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"list failed: {resp.status_code}"
    items = resp.json()["bookings"]
    assert any(b["ref"] == booking["ref"] for b in items), "fresh booking not in list"
    LOG.info("bookings tab ok: %d items", len(items))

    LOG.info("=== STEP 5: idempotency retry ===")
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", json=payload)
    booking_retry = resp.json()
    assert booking_retry["ref"] == booking["ref"], "idempotency failed"
    LOG.info("idempotency confirmed: ref unchanged")

    LOG.info("=== STEP 6: verify admin shows it ===")
    s = requests.Session()
    s.post(f"{base_url}/admin", data={"password": admin_password})
    resp = s.get(f"{base_url}/admin/bookings")
    assert booking["ref"] in resp.text, "ref not in admin"
    assert "🌐" in resp.text or "src-pwa" in resp.text, "PWA badge missing"
    LOG.info("admin parity ok")

    LOG.info("=== ALL STEPS PASSED ===")
    return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s")
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--base-url", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--admin-password", required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()
    sys.exit(0 if run(args.base_url, args.admin_password) else 1)

def test_full_booking_flow_localhost():
    \"\"\"pytest entrypoint — assumes local uvicorn on :8000.\"\"\"
    import os
    if os.environ.get("E2E_SKIP"):
        pytest.skip("E2E_SKIP set")
    run("http://localhost:8000", os.environ.get("E2E_ADMIN_PASSWORD", "test"))
```

# Detailed logging

Every STEP block emits a clearly delineated INFO log. Failures include the full response body in the log.

# CI integration

In CI, spin up uvicorn in a background process, run pytest with `E2E_SKIP=` (empty), tear down. In production smoke testing, invoke the script directly.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Script runnable as `python tests/e2e/test_full_booking_flow.py --base-url ... --admin-password ...`.
- [ ] Pytest entrypoint passes when uvicorn is reachable.
- [ ] Each step emits a clearly labeled log line.
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on any failure.
close_reason: E2E script landed in 155eee1. Six steps over real HTTP (httpx) covering bootstrap → validate_promo → create → list → idempotency → admin parity (incl. .src-pwa badge). Steps 4/5 degrade with WARN logs until br-4.12/4.13 wire bookings_token + client_request_id replay. Pytest entrypoint self-skips unless E2E_RUN=1, probes /health with 2s timeout. Runnable standalone as: python tests/e2e/test_full_booking_flow.py --base-url <url> --admin-password $ADMIN_PASSWORD.

title: E2E test script: token reuse across multiple bookings
description:
# Context

Verify token semantics end-to-end: a booking mints a token, a second booking with the same token echoes it back, a wrong token mints a fresh one.

# Script: tests/e2e/test_token_lifecycle.py

```python
\"\"\"E2E: token mint, reuse, and fresh-mint-on-mismatch.\"\"\"

import logging
import requests
import uuid

LOG = logging.getLogger("e2e.token_lifecycle")

def run(base_url: str) -> bool:
    test_phone = "212611200001"  # avoid colliding with other E2E tests

    LOG.info("=== Booking 1: no token sent, server mints fresh ===")
    payload = _build_payload(test_phone, crid=str(uuid.uuid4()))
    r1 = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", json=payload).json()
    token_1 = r1["bookings_token"]
    assert token_1, "no token returned"
    LOG.info("got token_1 ref=%s", r1["ref"])

    LOG.info("=== Booking 2: send token_1 back, server should echo ===")
    payload = _build_payload(test_phone, crid=str(uuid.uuid4()), bookings_token=token_1)
    r2 = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", json=payload).json()
    assert r2["bookings_token"] == token_1, "token NOT reused"
    LOG.info("token reused ref=%s", r2["ref"])

    LOG.info("=== Booking 3: send bogus token, server should mint fresh ===")
    payload = _build_payload(test_phone, crid=str(uuid.uuid4()), bookings_token="not-a-real-token-12345")
    r3 = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", json=payload).json()
    assert r3["bookings_token"] not in (token_1, "not-a-real-token-12345"), "fresh mint failed"
    token_3 = r3["bookings_token"]
    LOG.info("fresh token minted ref=%s", r3["ref"])

    LOG.info("=== GET /bookings with token_1 still works (old tokens stay valid) ===")
    resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": token_1})
    assert resp.status_code == 200, "old token revoked unexpectedly"
    LOG.info("old token still valid")

    LOG.info("=== GET /bookings with token from different phone returns its own bookings only ===")
    # would need second phone setup; for now just confirm same-phone path works

    LOG.info("=== ALL TOKEN LIFECYCLE STEPS PASSED ===")
    return True
```

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Script tests the 4 lifecycle steps.
- [ ] Each step logs success/failure with context.
- [ ] Runnable standalone and via pytest.
close_reason: Completed: added opt-in token lifecycle E2E script

title: E2E test script: rate limit burst smoke test
description:
# Context

A short script that hammers POST /bookings to verify the rate limiter fires correctly. Useful for production smoke testing after deploy.

# Script: tests/e2e/test_rate_limit_burst.py

```python
\"\"\"E2E: confirm POST /bookings rate-limits at 5/hour/phone.\"\"\"

import logging
import requests
import uuid

LOG = logging.getLogger("e2e.rate_limit_burst")

def run(base_url: str) -> bool:
    test_phone = "212611200099"  # avoid collisions
    results = []
    for i in range(10):
        crid = str(uuid.uuid4())
        payload = _build_payload(test_phone, crid=crid)
        resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", json=payload)
        results.append((i, resp.status_code, resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "-")))
        LOG.info("attempt %d → status=%d retry_after=%s", i, resp.status_code, resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "-"))

    success_count = sum(1 for _, s, _ in results if s == 200)
    rate_limited = sum(1 for _, s, _ in results if s == 429)
    LOG.info("=== burst summary: %d successes, %d rate-limited ===", success_count, rate_limited)

    assert success_count == 5, f"expected 5 successes (limit), got {success_count}"
    assert rate_limited == 5, f"expected 5 rate-limited (10-5), got {rate_limited}"
    assert all(r[2] != "-" for r in results if r[1] == 429), "rate-limited responses missing Retry-After header"

    LOG.info("=== RATE LIMIT BEHAVIOR CONFIRMED ===")
    return True
```

# When to run

- After every production deploy (smoke test).
- In CI as an integration check (with mocked clock to advance past 1 hour for cleanup).

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Script confirms 5/10 succeed and 5/10 are 429.
- [ ] Retry-After header present on every 429.
close_reason: Completed: opt-in E2E script verifies 5 successful bookings and 5 Retry-After 429s for rate-limit burst

title: Tests: update conftest.py with new env defaults
description:
# Context

`tests/conftest.py` currently sets 4 META_* env vars to placeholders so `app.config.settings` can import cleanly. The new milestone adds env vars that some tests need defaults for: ADMIN_PASSWORD (for admin route tests), EWASH_API_ENABLED (default true for API tests), ALLOWED_ORIGINS (empty by default), rate-limit overrides.

# Scope

Append to `tests/conftest.py`:

```python
import os

# Existing META_* placeholders ...

os.environ.setdefault("ADMIN_PASSWORD", "test-admin-password")
os.environ.setdefault("EWASH_API_ENABLED", "true")
os.environ.setdefault("ALLOWED_ORIGINS", "")  # empty — most tests don't exercise CORS
os.environ.setdefault("ALLOWED_ORIGIN_REGEX", "")
os.environ.setdefault("INTERNAL_CRON_SECRET", "test-cron-secret")

# Generous rate limits so unrelated tests aren't 429'd
os.environ.setdefault("RATE_LIMIT_BOOKINGS_PER_PHONE", "1000/hour")
os.environ.setdefault("RATE_LIMIT_BOOKINGS_PER_IP", "1000/hour")
os.environ.setdefault("RATE_LIMIT_PROMO_PER_IP", "1000/hour")
os.environ.setdefault("RATE_LIMIT_BOOKINGS_LIST_PER_TOKEN", "1000/hour")
```

# Why setdefault not unconditional

Some tests need to override these (test_api_rate_limit.py uses small limits; test_api_cors.py sets origins). `setdefault` means "if not already set" — those tests can still override via monkeypatch.

# Why generous defaults

The rate-limit-tests are the EXCEPTION. Default test posture is "rate limits effectively off" so other tests aren't accidentally rate-limited.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] conftest.py defaults documented.
- [ ] All existing tests pass.
- [ ] Rate-limit tests can still override.
close_reason: Completed: conftest.py seeds ADMIN_PASSWORD, INTERNAL_CRON_SECRET, EWASH_API_ENABLED, ALLOWED_ORIGINS, ALLOWED_ORIGIN_REGEX, RATE_LIMIT_*; test_config_defaults adapted to use monkeypatch.delenv; 78 tests pass.

title: E2E: cross-channel dedup automated script
description:
# Context

Automate what 6pa.8.6 documents as a manual procedure: PWA submits a booking, WhatsApp message arrives from the same phone, both attribute to one customer.

# Script: tests/e2e/test_cross_channel_dedup.py

```python
\"\"\"E2E: verify a PWA booking + WhatsApp message for the same phone dedupe.\"\"\"

import logging
import requests
import uuid
import json
import hmac
import hashlib
import os

LOG = logging.getLogger("e2e.cross_channel_dedup")

def run(base_url: str, meta_app_secret: str) -> bool:
    test_phone_pwa = "+212 6 11 20 88 99"
    test_phone_norm = "212611208899"
    crid = str(uuid.uuid4())

    LOG.info("=== STEP 1: POST a booking from PWA-style payload ===")
    payload = {
        "phone": test_phone_pwa,
        "name": "DedupTest",
        "category": "A",
        "vehicle": {"make": "Sentra", "color": "Jaune"},
        "location": {"kind": "home", "pin_address": "Test Casablanca"},
        "service_id": "svc_ext",
        "date": "2026-12-15",
        "slot": "slot_14_16",
        "addon_ids": [],
        "client_request_id": crid,
    }
    r = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", json=payload)
    assert r.status_code == 200, f"create failed: {r.text}"
    booking = r.json()
    LOG.info("PWA booking created ref=%s", booking["ref"])

    LOG.info("=== STEP 2: simulate a WhatsApp inbound from the same normalized phone ===")
    webhook_payload = {
        "object": "whatsapp_business_account",
        "entry": [{
            "id": "test", "changes": [{
                "value": {
                    "messages": [{
                        "from": test_phone_norm,
                        "id": "wamid.test_" + uuid.uuid4().hex,
                        "timestamp": "1747000000",
                        "type": "text",
                        "text": {"body": "Bonjour"},
                    }],
                    "contacts": [{"wa_id": test_phone_norm, "profile": {"name": "DedupTest WA"}}],
                },
                "field": "messages",
            }],
        }],
    }
    raw = json.dumps(webhook_payload, separators=(",", ":")).encode()
    sig = "sha256=" + hmac.new(meta_app_secret.encode(), raw, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
    r = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/webhook",
        data=raw,
        headers={"X-Hub-Signature-256": sig, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
    )
    assert r.status_code == 200, f"webhook failed: {r.text}"
    LOG.info("WhatsApp inbound delivered to webhook")

    LOG.info("=== STEP 3: verify customer row count ===")
    # Use admin to verify — would need an admin session OR a direct DB query.
    # For now, instruct the operator running this script to check /admin/customers.
    LOG.info("Manual check: /admin/customers should show ONE row for phone %s with both names accumulated", test_phone_norm)

    LOG.info("=== STEP 4: verify returning-customer prompt was sent ===")
    LOG.info("Manual check: the WhatsApp test number should have received 'Bonjour DedupTest, est-ce pour Sentra — Jaune ?' prompt")

    LOG.info("=== DEDUP STEPS COMPLETED — operator confirms manual checks ===")
    return True

if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s")
    import argparse
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("--base-url", required=True)
    p.add_argument("--meta-app-secret", required=True, help="To sign the webhook payload")
    args = p.parse_args()
    run(args.base_url, args.meta_app_secret)
```

# Why "manual check" at the end

Verifying customer table state requires DB access OR an admin endpoint that returns it. Since this is an E2E test against live infra, we ask the operator to confirm in /admin/customers. A future improvement: add `GET /api/v1/_e2e/customer_count?phone=...` gated by a hardened secret for full automation.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Script runs and produces all 4 STEP logs.
- [ ] Webhook signature verification passes (Meta-style HMAC).
- [ ] Operator confirms /admin/customers shows the single row.
close_reason: Completed: opt-in cross-channel E2E posts PWA booking, signs WhatsApp webhook, and logs manual dedup checks

title: Test: tests/test_access_log_middleware.py — pin the structured log contract
description:
# Context

The access-log middleware (6pa.2.5) is load-bearing for ops debugging. Every `/api/*` request MUST emit exactly one log line with all the documented fields. If a future refactor breaks this contract, we'd lose observability silently. Pin it.

# Test functions

```python
import logging
import re
import pytest
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

@pytest.fixture
def caplog_api(caplog):
    caplog.set_level(logging.INFO, logger="ewash.api")
    return caplog

def test_every_api_request_emits_one_log_line(client, caplog_api):
    client.get("/api/v1/catalog/categories")
    matches = [r for r in caplog_api.records if r.name == "ewash.api" and "/api/v1/catalog/categories" in r.getMessage()]
    assert len(matches) == 1, f"expected exactly 1 log line, got {len(matches)}"

def test_log_line_has_all_documented_fields(client, caplog_api):
    client.get("/api/v1/catalog/categories")
    msg = next(r.getMessage() for r in caplog_api.records if "/api/v1/catalog/categories" in r.getMessage())
    # Required fields
    for field in ("endpoint=", "method=", "status=", "duration_ms=", "phone_hash=", "source_ip_hash="):
        assert field in msg, f"log missing field: {field}"

def test_log_line_phone_hash_only_when_phone_known(client, caplog_api):
    # GET /categories has no phone context
    client.get("/api/v1/catalog/categories")
    msg = next(r.getMessage() for r in caplog_api.records if "/api/v1/catalog/categories" in r.getMessage())
    assert "phone_hash=-" in msg, "phone_hash should be '-' when no phone context"

def test_log_line_includes_ref_after_booking_create(client, caplog_api):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_valid_payload())
    ref = resp.json()["ref"]
    msg = next(r.getMessage() for r in caplog_api.records if "/api/v1/bookings" in r.getMessage())
    assert f"ref={ref}" in msg, "ref should appear after booking handler stashes it"

def test_log_line_includes_error_code_on_4xx(client, caplog_api):
    # Submit invalid payload
    bad = _valid_payload()
    bad["category"] = "Z"  # invalid
    client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=bad)
    msg = next(r.getMessage() for r in caplog_api.records if "/api/v1/bookings" in r.getMessage())
    assert "status=422" in msg
    assert "error_code=" in msg
    # Either Pydantic validation_error or our domain code
    assert ("error_code=validation_error" in msg) or ("error_code=invalid_category" in msg)

def test_phone_hash_never_contains_raw_phone(client, caplog_api):
    client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_valid_payload(phone="212611204502"))
    for r in caplog_api.records:
        # NO raw phone digits anywhere in log lines (security check)
        assert "212611204502" not in r.getMessage(), "raw phone leaked to logs"

def test_duration_ms_reasonable(client, caplog_api):
    client.get("/api/v1/catalog/categories")
    msg = next(r.getMessage() for r in caplog_api.records if "/api/v1/catalog/categories" in r.getMessage())
    m = re.search(r"duration_ms=(\\d+\\.\\d+)", msg)
    assert m, "duration_ms missing"
    duration = float(m.group(1))
    assert 0 < duration < 5000, f"unreasonable duration: {duration}"

def test_5xx_logged_at_info_not_error_level(client, caplog_api, monkeypatch):
    # 5xx in the access log should still be INFO (operational, not application error)
    # The application-level error (the exception itself) is logged separately at ERROR.
    def boom(*a, **kw): raise RuntimeError("simulated")
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.api.endpoint_categories", boom)
    # ... assert exactly one INFO line + one ERROR line ...

def test_health_endpoint_NOT_logged_by_api_middleware(client, caplog_api):
    \"\"\"The /health endpoint is outside /api/ so the API access log skips it.\"\"\"
    client.get("/health")
    matches = [r for r in caplog_api.records if r.name == "ewash.api" and "/health" in r.getMessage()]
    assert len(matches) == 0, "API middleware should not log /health"

def test_webhook_endpoint_NOT_logged_by_api_middleware(client, caplog_api):
    \"\"\"The /webhook endpoint is outside /api/ — WhatsApp path has its own logging.\"\"\"
    client.get("/webhook", params={"hub.mode": "subscribe", "hub.verify_token": "wrong", "hub.challenge": "x"})
    matches = [r for r in caplog_api.records if r.name == "ewash.api" and "/webhook" in r.getMessage()]
    assert len(matches) == 0
```

# Why these specific assertions

- **Exactly one log line** — catches the "we accidentally logged twice from two middleware layers" regression.
- **All documented fields** — catches the "we removed phone_hash to save log volume" regression.
- **No raw phone** — catches the "someone accidentally moved phone_hash to use raw phone" regression (data leak).
- **Reasonable duration** — catches the "the t0 timing got reset somewhere" regression.
- **Health/webhook skipped** — catches the "middleware now applies to everything" regression.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 9 tests pass.
- [ ] No raw PII (phone, IP) appears in any log line.
- [ ] /health and /webhook are NOT logged by the API middleware.
close_reason: Completed: structured access log contract pinned with 11 focused tests.

title: Test: tests/test_feature_flag.py — pin EWASH_API_ENABLED behavior
description:
# Context

The feature flag (6pa.2.7) is our emergency rollback switch. We need an automated test that asserts:
1. When the flag is ON, /api/v1/* is reachable.
2. When the flag is OFF, /api/v1/* returns 404 (router not mounted).
3. Either way, the WhatsApp /webhook path and /admin paths are unaffected.
4. Either way, /health works.

# Test approach

The flag is read at app import / startup. To test both states without restarting the test process, we create fresh app instances using FastAPI's factory pattern, OR we patch the setting and use a fresh TestClient.

```python
import pytest
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

def _make_app(api_enabled: bool):
    \"\"\"Construct a fresh FastAPI app with the flag set.\"\"\"
    import importlib, app.config, app.main
    app.config.settings.api_enabled = api_enabled
    importlib.reload(app.main)
    return app.main.app

def test_api_router_mounted_when_flag_true(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.api_enabled", True)
    a = _make_app(True)
    c = TestClient(a)
    resp = c.get("/api/v1/catalog/categories")
    assert resp.status_code == 200

def test_api_router_unmounted_when_flag_false(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.api_enabled", False)
    a = _make_app(False)
    c = TestClient(a)
    resp = c.get("/api/v1/catalog/categories")
    assert resp.status_code == 404

def test_webhook_works_regardless_of_flag(monkeypatch):
    for state in (True, False):
        monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.api_enabled", state)
        a = _make_app(state)
        c = TestClient(a)
        resp = c.get("/webhook", params={"hub.mode": "subscribe", "hub.verify_token": "wrong", "hub.challenge": "x"})
        # Returns 403 (signature mismatch) — but NOT 404 (path exists)
        assert resp.status_code in (200, 403), f"webhook broken when api_enabled={state}"

def test_admin_works_regardless_of_flag(monkeypatch):
    for state in (True, False):
        monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.api_enabled", state)
        a = _make_app(state)
        c = TestClient(a)
        resp = c.get("/admin")
        # Admin login form or 503 (no password set) — either is acceptable
        assert resp.status_code in (200, 401, 503), f"admin broken when api_enabled={state}"

def test_health_works_regardless_of_flag(monkeypatch):
    for state in (True, False):
        monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.api_enabled", state)
        a = _make_app(state)
        c = TestClient(a)
        resp = c.get("/health")
        assert resp.status_code == 200

def test_cors_middleware_skipped_when_flag_false(monkeypatch):
    \"\"\"When API is off, CORS middleware should not bother running either.

    Verify by sending an OPTIONS preflight — should NOT get CORS headers back
    even from an allowed origin, because the whole API surface is gone.
    \"\"\"
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.api_enabled", False)
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.allowed_origins", "https://allowed.com")
    a = _make_app(False)
    c = TestClient(a)
    resp = c.options("/api/v1/bootstrap", headers={"Origin": "https://allowed.com"})
    # Either 404 (no route) or empty CORS headers
    assert resp.status_code == 404 or "access-control-allow-origin" not in {k.lower() for k in resp.headers}

def test_startup_log_reflects_flag_state(monkeypatch, caplog):
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.api_enabled", True)
    with caplog.at_level("INFO", logger="ewash"):
        _make_app(True)
    assert any("enabled" in r.getMessage() and "/api/v1" in r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records)

    caplog.clear()
    monkeypatch.setattr("app.config.settings.api_enabled", False)
    with caplog.at_level("WARNING", logger="ewash"):
        _make_app(False)
    assert any("disabled" in r.getMessage() and "/api/v1" in r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records)
```

# Why test BOTH states explicitly

The CI default has api_enabled=True. The Off state is only exercised manually. Without this test, the disabled-flag path could rot — a future refactor breaks the conditional mount, nobody notices because no test exercises it, then the rollback drill fails when you need it most.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 7 tests pass.
- [ ] Flag-off path tested mechanically, not just manually.
- [ ] Startup logs explicit message reflecting flag state.
close_reason: Completed: feature flag on/off behavior pinned with seven focused tests.

title: Test: tests/load/test_concurrent_bookings.py — race-safety load test
description:
# Context

`assign_booking_ref` uses `with_for_update()` on `booking_ref_counters` to prevent concurrent refs from colliding. We have a single-process unit test (in 6pa.7.4) that fires 5 threads. This bead is a MORE thorough load test: 50 concurrent POSTs, asserting all refs are distinct and the counter advances by exactly 50.

Also tests:
- Idempotency under concurrent retries of the same client_request_id.
- Rate limit behavior under burst (separate from logical happy-path).
- Transaction atomicity under contention.

# Script: tests/load/test_concurrent_bookings.py

```python
\"\"\"Load test: many concurrent POSTs, verify race-safety properties.

Run with: pytest -m load tests/load/test_concurrent_bookings.py -v
Or:       python tests/load/test_concurrent_bookings.py --base-url http://localhost:8000
\"\"\"

import argparse
import logging
import concurrent.futures
import uuid
import requests
import sys

LOG = logging.getLogger("load.concurrent_bookings")
LOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)

def _post(base_url, payload):
    r = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/v1/bookings", json=payload, timeout=15)
    return r.status_code, r.json() if r.status_code < 500 else {"error": r.text}

def run_distinct_refs(base_url: str, n: int = 50) -> bool:
    \"\"\"N concurrent distinct phones → N distinct refs, no errors.\"\"\"
    LOG.info("=== distinct refs: firing %d concurrent POSTs ===", n)
    def make_payload(i):
        return {
            "phone": f"21261120{i:04d}",
            "name": f"LoadTest{i}",
            "category": "A",
            "vehicle": {"make": "Clio", "color": "Bleu"},
            "location": {"kind": "home", "pin_address": "Load Test"},
            "service_id": "svc_ext",
            "date": "2026-12-01",
            "slot": "slot_10_12",
            "addon_ids": [],
            "client_request_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        }
    payloads = [make_payload(i) for i in range(n)]
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=20) as ex:
        results = list(ex.map(lambda p: _post(base_url, p), payloads))
    successes = [r for r in results if r[0] == 200]
    refs = [r[1]["ref"] for r in successes]
    LOG.info("got %d successes / %d", len(successes), n)
    assert len(successes) == n, f"only {len(successes)}/{n} successful"
    assert len(set(refs)) == n, f"ref collision: {len(set(refs))} unique out of {n}"
    LOG.info("=== all %d refs distinct ===", n)
    return True

def run_idempotency_under_contention(base_url: str) -> bool:
    \"\"\"Fire 10 concurrent POSTs with the SAME crid + phone → 10 returns with SAME ref.\"\"\"
    LOG.info("=== idempotency contention: 10 concurrent POSTs same crid ===")
    crid = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "phone": "212611299988",
        "name": "ContentionTest",
        "category": "A",
        "vehicle": {"make": "Clio", "color": "Bleu"},
        "location": {"kind": "home", "pin_address": "X"},
        "service_id": "svc_ext",
        "date": "2026-12-01",
        "slot": "slot_10_12",
        "addon_ids": [],
        "client_request_id": crid,
    }
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=10) as ex:
        results = list(ex.map(lambda _: _post(base_url, payload), range(10)))
    refs = [r[1].get("ref") for r in results if r[0] == 200]
    assert all(r is not None for r in refs), f"some failed: {results}"
    assert len(set(refs)) == 1, f"idempotency broken: {set(refs)}"
    LOG.info("=== all 10 returned same ref: %s ===", refs[0])
    return True

def run_rate_limit_burst(base_url: str) -> bool:
    \"\"\"50 POSTs from same phone — should see exactly RATE_LIMIT_BOOKINGS_PER_PHONE 200s and rest 429s.\"\"\"
    LOG.info("=== rate limit burst: 50 POSTs same phone ===")
    def payload(i):
        return {
            "phone": "212611288877",  # all same phone — but distinct crids
            "name": "RateTest",
            "category": "A",
            "vehicle": {"make": "Clio", "color": "Bleu"},
            "location": {"kind": "home", "pin_address": "X"},
            "service_id": "svc_ext",
            "date": "2026-12-01",
            "slot": "slot_10_12",
            "addon_ids": [],
            "client_request_id": f"rate-{i}-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
        }
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=10) as ex:
        results = list(ex.map(lambda i: _post(base_url, payload(i)), range(50)))
    status_counts = {}
    for status, _ in results:
        status_counts[status] = status_counts.get(status, 0) + 1
    LOG.info("status distribution: %s", status_counts)
    assert 200 in status_counts and 429 in status_counts, "expected mix of 200 + 429"
    return True

def main():
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("--base-url", default="http://localhost:8000")
    p.add_argument("--scenario", choices=["distinct", "idempotent", "rate_limit", "all"], default="all")
    args = p.parse_args()
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s")
    scenarios = [args.scenario] if args.scenario != "all" else ["distinct", "idempotent", "rate_limit"]
    for s in scenarios:
        try:
            if s == "distinct": run_distinct_refs(args.base_url, n=50)
            elif s == "idempotent": run_idempotency_under_contention(args.base_url)
            elif s == "rate_limit": run_rate_limit_burst(args.base_url)
        except AssertionError as e:
            LOG.error("scenario %s FAILED: %s", s, e)
            sys.exit(1)
    LOG.info("=== ALL LOAD SCENARIOS PASSED ===")
    sys.exit(0)

# Pytest entrypoint (marked with `load` so default pytest skips)
import pytest
@pytest.mark.load
def test_distinct_refs_under_load():
    \"\"\"Spawns concurrent threads against in-process TestClient.\"\"\"
    # ... uses TestClient instead of requests; same logic ...
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

# Why run this both in-process AND against a real instance

- **In-process (TestClient)**: SQLAlchemy session-level locking. Catches Python-level race conditions.
- **Against real Postgres**: catches DB-level race conditions (the `with_for_update` is the real test here).
- The CI runs the in-process version. Manual smoke before production runs the real-instance version.

# `pytest -m load` marker

Add to `pyproject.toml` or `pytest.ini`:

```ini
[pytest]
markers =
    load: marks tests as load tests (deselected by default; run with -m load)
```

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 3 scenarios pass.
- [ ] No flakiness across 10 consecutive runs.
- [ ] Idempotency holds under contention (10 concurrent same-crid → 1 unique ref).
- [ ] Race on counter holds (50 concurrent distinct phones → 50 unique refs).
- [ ] Rate limit cap holds under burst.
close_reason: Completed: added opt-in concurrent booking load tests for refs, idempotency, and rate limits

title: Test: tests/test_api_tokens_revoke.py — revoke endpoint behavior
description:
# Context

Pin the contract for `POST /api/v1/tokens/revoke` (6pa.4.17). Token revocation is security-critical — a regression that leaves revoked tokens valid is exactly the bug we'd want to catch automatically.

# Test functions

```python
import pytest
from sqlalchemy import select
from app.models import CustomerTokenRow
from app.persistence import mint_customer_token

def test_revoke_current_removes_just_this_token(client, db_engine):
    # Mint 2 tokens for the same phone (simulate two devices).
    t1 = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    t2 = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    # Revoke t1 via API.
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t1}, json={"scope": "current"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["revoked_count"] == 1
    # t1 invalid, t2 still valid.
    assert client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t1}).status_code == 401
    assert client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t2}).status_code == 200

def test_revoke_all_removes_every_token(client, db_engine):
    t1 = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    t2 = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    t3 = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t1}, json={"scope": "all"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["revoked_count"] == 3
    # all 3 tokens gone
    for t in (t1, t2, t3):
        assert client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}).status_code == 401
    # And confirm DB
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        rows = sess.execute(select(CustomerTokenRow).where(CustomerTokenRow.customer_phone == "212611204502")).scalars().all()
        assert rows == []

def test_revoke_missing_token_returns_401(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", json={"scope": "current"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    assert resp.json()["error_code"] == "missing_token"

def test_revoke_invalid_token_returns_401(client):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": "bogus"}, json={"scope": "current"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    assert resp.json()["error_code"] == "invalid_token"

def test_revoke_default_scope_is_current(client, db_engine):
    t1 = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    t2 = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    # Body without explicit scope → defaults to current
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t1}, json={})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["revoked_count"] == 1
    # t2 unaffected
    assert client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t2}).status_code == 200

def test_revoke_extra_fields_rejected(client):
    \"\"\"Pydantic extra='forbid' should reject unknown fields.\"\"\"
    t = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"scope": "current", "bogus": "x"})
    assert resp.status_code == 422

def test_revoke_invalid_scope_rejected(client):
    t = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"scope": "whatever"})
    assert resp.status_code == 422

def test_revoke_rate_limited(client, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(settings, "rate_limit_promo_per_ip", "3/hour")  # piggyback any per-IP limit
    # Make 11 attempts; expect the 11th to be 429 (limit is 10/hour per token).
    t = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    for i in range(10):
        client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"scope": "current"})
    # The 11th. But wait — after the first successful one, the token is gone.
    # In practice the test asserts behavior under retries (against a fresh token).
    # Refactor: mint a token, hit revoke 10 times in a row (all 401 after the first since token is gone),
    # then a 11th time, assert 429 from per-token rate limit (which keys on the token hash including invalid ones).
    # The rate limit applies to the endpoint regardless of token validity.

def test_revoke_emits_audit_log(client, caplog):
    t = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    with caplog.at_level("INFO", logger="ewash.api"):
        client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"scope": "current"})
    msgs = [r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records]
    assert any("tokens.revoked" in m and "scope=current" in m and "count=1" in m for m in msgs)

def test_revoke_all_mints_fresh_token_in_response(client):
    \"\"\"When scope=all, the response includes a freshly-minted token so the
    requesting device stays logged in. (Per the design in 6pa.4.17 / 6pa.6.15.)\"\"\"
    t1 = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t1}, json={"scope": "all"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    new_token = resp.json().get("new_token")
    assert new_token, "scope=all must mint a fresh token for the requesting device"
    assert new_token != t1
    # New token works
    assert client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": new_token}).status_code == 200

def test_revoke_audit_log_includes_phone_hash_not_phone(client, caplog):
    t = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    with caplog.at_level("INFO", logger="ewash.api"):
        client.post("/api/v1/tokens/revoke", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"scope": "current"})
    msgs = " ".join(r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records)
    assert "212611204502" not in msgs, "raw phone leaked to logs"
```

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 10 tests pass.
- [ ] Revoked tokens are immediately invalid (no caching/race window).
- [ ] scope=all path mints a fresh token for the requesting device.
- [ ] Audit log includes phone_hash + scope + count, NO raw phone.
- [ ] Rate-limited per-token.
close_reason: Completed: token revoke tests cover current/all scopes, invalid tokens, rate limiting, audit logs, and scope=all fresh token

title: Test: tests/test_api_me_delete.py — data erasure compliance
description:
# Context

Pin the `DELETE /api/v1/me` contract (6pa.4.18). Data deletion is compliance-critical (Loi 09-08 / GDPR). A regression that fails to wipe a single field could be a regulatory issue. Tests must be exhaustive about what's deleted, what's anonymized, and what's preserved.

# Test functions

```python
import pytest
import hashlib
from sqlalchemy import select
from app.models import (
    Customer, CustomerName, CustomerTokenRow, CustomerVehicle,
    BookingRow, ConversationSessionRow, ConversationEventRow,
)

def test_delete_me_requires_token(client):
    resp = client.delete("/api/v1/me", json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    assert resp.json()["error_code"] == "missing_token"

def test_delete_me_requires_valid_token(client):
    resp = client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": "bogus"}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    assert resp.json()["error_code"] == "invalid_token"

def test_delete_me_requires_literal_confirm_phrase(client):
    t = mint_customer_token("212611204502")
    # Wrong phrase
    resp = client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "yes"})
    assert resp.status_code == 422  # Pydantic Literal rejection
    # Empty body
    resp = client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={})
    assert resp.status_code == 422

def test_delete_me_purges_tokens(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"All customer_tokens for this phone are deleted.\"\"\"
    phone = "212611204502"
    t1 = mint_customer_token(phone)
    t2 = mint_customer_token(phone)
    t3 = mint_customer_token(phone)
    resp = client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t1}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    # All tokens gone
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        rows = sess.execute(select(CustomerTokenRow).where(CustomerTokenRow.customer_phone == phone)).all()
        assert rows == []

def test_delete_me_purges_names(client, db_engine):
    phone = "212611204502"
    persistence.persist_customer_name(phone, "Hassan")
    persistence.persist_customer_name(phone, "Youssef")
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        rows = sess.execute(select(CustomerName).where(CustomerName.customer_phone == phone)).all()
        assert rows == []

def test_delete_me_purges_vehicles(client, db_engine):
    phone = "212611204502"
    # Seed a vehicle via WhatsApp booking flow or directly
    ...
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        rows = sess.execute(select(CustomerVehicle).where(CustomerVehicle.customer_phone == phone)).all()
        assert rows == []

def test_delete_me_purges_conversation_sessions(client, db_engine):
    phone = "212611204502"
    persistence.persist_customer_bot_stage(phone, stage="MENU", label="Menu affiché")
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        sessions = sess.execute(select(ConversationSessionRow).where(ConversationSessionRow.customer_phone == phone)).all()
        events = sess.execute(select(ConversationEventRow).where(ConversationEventRow.customer_phone == phone)).all()
        assert sessions == [] and events == []

def test_delete_me_anonymizes_bookings(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"Booking rows are PRESERVED with PII scrubbed.\"\"\"
    phone = "212611204502"
    booking = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_valid_payload(phone=phone, name="TestUser", note="ring twice")).json()
    t = booking["bookings_token"]
    client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    # Find the anonymized booking
    anon_phone = "DEL-" + hashlib.sha256(phone.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        anon_booking = sess.scalar(select(BookingRow).where(BookingRow.ref == booking["ref"]))
        assert anon_booking is not None  # row preserved
        assert anon_booking.customer_phone == anon_phone
        assert anon_booking.customer_name == "Anonyme"
        assert anon_booking.car_model == ""
        assert anon_booking.color == ""
        assert anon_booking.address == ""
        assert anon_booking.note == ""
        assert anon_booking.latitude is None
        assert anon_booking.longitude is None
        assert anon_booking.raw_booking_json == "{}"
        # But aggregate fields preserved
        assert anon_booking.ref.startswith("EW-")
        assert anon_booking.status  # status enum value preserved
        assert anon_booking.total_price_dh > 0
        assert anon_booking.appointment_date is not None
        assert anon_booking.service_id  # for analytics

def test_delete_me_response_counts(client):
    phone = "212611204502"
    # Setup: 2 tokens, 1 name, 1 vehicle, 1 booking, 1 conversation session
    ...
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    resp = client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    data = resp.json()
    # deleted_count = tokens (3) + names (1) + vehicles (1) + sessions (1) = 6
    assert data["deleted_count"] == 6
    assert data["anonymized_bookings"] == 1

def test_delete_me_audit_row_created(client, db_engine, caplog):
    phone = "212611204502"
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    with caplog.at_level("INFO", logger="ewash.api"):
        client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    # Audit table row
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        rows = sess.execute(select(DataErasureAuditRow).order_by(DataErasureAuditRow.performed_at.desc())).scalars().all()
        assert len(rows) >= 1
        assert rows[0].actor == "customer_self_serve"
        assert rows[0].deleted_count > 0
    # And the audit log
    msgs = [r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records]
    assert any("me.delete" in m for m in msgs)

def test_delete_me_log_never_contains_raw_phone(client, caplog):
    phone = "212611204502"
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    with caplog.at_level("INFO", logger="ewash.api"):
        client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    all_messages = " ".join(r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records)
    assert "212611204502" not in all_messages, "raw phone leaked to audit log"

def test_delete_me_does_not_affect_other_customers(client, db_engine):
    # Customer A and B both have data.
    t_a = ...  # for phone A
    client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t_a}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    # B's data is intact
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        assert sess.scalar(select(Customer).where(Customer.phone == "212611200002")) is not None
        assert sess.scalar(select(CustomerName).where(CustomerName.customer_phone == "212611200002")) is not None

def test_delete_me_after_delete_token_invalid(client):
    phone = "212611204502"
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    # Try to use the same token again
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    assert resp.json()["error_code"] == "invalid_token"

def test_delete_me_idempotent_on_second_call(client):
    \"\"\"Second delete on already-deleted customer returns 401 (token no longer valid).\"\"\"
    phone = "212611204502"
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    # The token is gone — second call returns 401
    resp = client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401

def test_delete_me_rate_limited(client, monkeypatch):
    # 4th call from same token in an hour → 429.
    # (Tokens get deleted on first success — so test needs a token that fails to
    # actually delete OR test the rate limit BEFORE the first valid call.)
    # Practical approach: mint 4 tokens for same phone; call delete-me 4 times.
    # Each call invalidates one token but the limit keys on the token-hash so each is independent.
    # Wait — that means rate limit is per-token, not per-phone. Re-evaluate.
    # Per design: 3/hour PER TOKEN. So 4 calls with the SAME token: first succeeds,
    # subsequent 401 (token gone). 4 calls with DIFFERENT tokens: all might succeed
    # (each token is a separate rate-limit key).
    # Better: keep the rate limit per the IP that's hitting the endpoint.
    # OR per-phone (need to verify the token first, then key on phone).
    # For simplicity, key on IP. Add to bead.
    pass

def test_delete_me_logs_phone_hash_consistent_with_audit(client, db_engine, caplog):
    \"\"\"The phone_hash in logs matches the audit row's phone_hash.\"\"\"
    phone = "212611204502"
    t = mint_customer_token(phone)
    expected_hash = hashlib.sha256(phone.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
    with caplog.at_level("INFO", logger="ewash.api"):
        client.delete("/api/v1/me", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": t}, json={"confirm": "I confirm I want to delete my data"})
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        audit_row = sess.scalar(select(DataErasureAuditRow).order_by(DataErasureAuditRow.performed_at.desc()))
        assert audit_row.phone_hash == expected_hash
```

# E2E (separate script — tests/e2e/test_data_erasure.py)

```python
\"\"\"E2E: customer-initiated data erasure end-to-end.\"\"\"
def run(base_url: str) -> bool:
    # 1. Create a booking (gets a token).
    # 2. Verify booking visible via GET /bookings.
    # 3. DELETE /me.
    # 4. Verify GET /bookings returns 401.
    # 5. Verify the booking ref still exists in admin (anonymized).
    # All steps emit detailed logs.
```

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 15 unit tests pass.
- [ ] E2E script passes.
- [ ] No raw phone numbers in logs or audit rows.
- [ ] Audit row created for every successful deletion.
- [ ] Bookings preserved with PII scrubbed.
- [ ] Token gone after deletion (subsequent calls 401).

title: Test: tests/test_api_bookings_create.py — happy path + persistence + BG task + idempotency + multi-addon + token
description:
# Context

The big POST /bookings test file. Covers happy path, persistence integration, BackgroundTask scheduling, idempotency, multi-addon, token flow, AND the transaction-atomicity guarantee + line-item ordering.

# Test functions

```python
def test_happy_path_creates_booking_row(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"Submit a valid payload; verify BookingRow + line items + status event.\"\"\"
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["ref"].startswith("EW-")
    # DB asserts
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        row = sess.scalar(select(BookingRow).where(BookingRow.ref == body["ref"]))
        assert row is not None
        assert row.source == "api"
        assert row.client_request_id == _payload()["client_request_id"]
        # Main line item exists
        mains = [li for li in row.line_items if li.kind == "main"]
        assert len(mains) == 1
        # Status event recorded
        assert any(e.to_status == "pending_ewash_confirmation" for e in row.status_events)

def test_ref_pattern_and_monotonic_increment(client):
    r1 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid="a"))
    r2 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid="b"))
    n1 = int(r1.json()["ref"].split("-")[-1])
    n2 = int(r2.json()["ref"].split("-")[-1])
    assert n2 == n1 + 1, "ref counter must monotonically increment"
    assert r1.json()["ref"].startswith("EW-")

def test_source_is_api(client, db_engine):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        row = sess.scalar(select(BookingRow).where(BookingRow.ref == resp.json()["ref"]))
        assert row.source == "api"

def test_customer_phone_normalized(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"+212 6 11 20 45 02 → 212611204502.\"\"\"
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(phone="+212 6 11 20 45 02"))
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        row = sess.scalar(select(BookingRow).where(BookingRow.ref == resp.json()["ref"]))
        assert row.customer_phone == "212611204502"

def test_customer_names_history_updated(client, db_engine):
    client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(name="Foo"))
    client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(name="Bar"))
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        names = sess.execute(select(CustomerName).where(CustomerName.customer_phone == "212611204502")).scalars().all()
        labels = [n.display_name for n in names]
        assert "Foo" in labels and "Bar" in labels

def test_pricing_matches_catalog_exhaustively(client):
    \"\"\"Cross-reference response.price_dh with catalog.service_price() for all triples.\"\"\"
    for category in ("A", "B", "C"):
        for service_id in ("svc_ext", "svc_cpl", "svc_sal"):
            for promo in (None, "YS26"):
                resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(category=category, service_id=service_id, promo_code=promo))
                expected = catalog.service_price(service_id, category, promo_code=promo)
                assert resp.json()["price_dh"] == expected, f"price drift: {service_id}/{category}/{promo}"

def test_notification_scheduled_not_awaited(client, monkeypatch):
    \"\"\"BackgroundTask scheduled — assert the notify function is queued, not synchronously called.\"\"\"
    spy = MagicMock()
    monkeypatch.setattr(persistence, "notify_booking_confirmation_safe", spy)
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    # TestClient runs BackgroundTasks AFTER the response is sent.
    # By the time .json() returns, the task has either run or is queued.
    # We assert it WAS called (FastAPI TestClient runs BG tasks synchronously after response).
    assert spy.called
    assert spy.call_args.kwargs["booking_ref"] == resp.json()["ref"]

def test_notification_failure_does_not_break_api(client, monkeypatch):
    \"\"\"If notify_booking_confirmation raises, API still 200 + row persisted.\"\"\"
    def boom(**kwargs): raise RuntimeError("Meta is down")
    monkeypatch.setattr(persistence, "notify_booking_confirmation_safe", boom)
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    assert resp.status_code == 200  # response is independent of BG task outcome
    # The BG task ran AFTER the response so its exception just gets logged.

def test_transaction_atomicity_on_persist_failure(client, monkeypatch, db_engine):
    \"\"\"If persist_customer_name fails mid-handler, the BookingRow is rolled back.\"\"\"
    monkeypatch.setattr(persistence, "persist_customer_name",
                        lambda *a, **kw: (_ for _ in ()).throw(RuntimeError("simulated failure")))
    pre_count = _count_bookings(db_engine)
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    assert resp.status_code == 500
    post_count = _count_bookings(db_engine)
    assert pre_count == post_count, "BookingRow leaked despite failed name persistence"

def test_multi_addon_line_items_ordered(client, db_engine):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(addon_ids=["svc_cuir", "svc_plastq", "svc_optq"]))
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        row = sess.scalar(select(BookingRow).where(BookingRow.ref == resp.json()["ref"]))
        items = sorted(row.line_items, key=lambda li: li.sort_order)
        # main first (sort_order=0), then addons in submission order
        assert items[0].kind == "main"
        assert [li.service_id for li in items[1:]] == ["svc_cuir", "svc_plastq", "svc_optq"]
        # First addon denormalized into legacy fields
        assert row.addon_service == "svc_cuir"

def test_single_addon_denormalizes_to_legacy(client, db_engine):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(addon_ids=["svc_cuir"]))
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        row = sess.scalar(select(BookingRow).where(BookingRow.ref == resp.json()["ref"]))
        assert row.addon_service == "svc_cuir"
        assert row.addon_service_label
        assert row.addon_price_dh > 0

def test_addon_pricing_minus_10pct_round_half_to_even(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"Matches handlers.py:773 round(price * 0.9). Banker's rounding (Python default).\"\"\"
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(addon_ids=["svc_cuir"]))
    expected_addon = round(catalog.public_service_price("svc_cuir", "A") * 0.9)
    line_items = resp.json()["line_items"]
    addon_item = next(li for li in line_items if li["kind"] == "addon")
    assert addon_item["price_dh"] == expected_addon

def test_idempotency_same_crid_same_body_returns_original(client):
    r1 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid="aaa"))
    r2 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid="aaa"))
    assert r1.json()["ref"] == r2.json()["ref"]
    assert r2.json()["is_idempotent_replay"] is True

def test_idempotency_same_crid_different_body_returns_original(client):
    r1 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid="bbb", service_id="svc_ext"))
    r2 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid="bbb", service_id="svc_cpl"))
    assert r1.json()["ref"] == r2.json()["ref"]
    assert r2.json()["service_label"] == r1.json()["service_label"]  # ORIGINAL service, not new

def test_idempotency_no_crid_two_distinct_writes(client):
    r1 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid=None))
    r2 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid=None))
    assert r1.json()["ref"] != r2.json()["ref"]

def test_token_minted_on_first_booking(client, db_engine):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    token = resp.json()["bookings_token"]
    assert token and len(token) >= 43
    # Verify hash in customer_tokens
    digest = hash_token(token)
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        row = sess.scalar(select(CustomerTokenRow).where(CustomerTokenRow.token_hash == digest))
        assert row is not None
        assert row.customer_phone == "212611204502"

def test_token_reused_when_valid(client):
    r1 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    token = r1.json()["bookings_token"]
    r2 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(bookings_token=token))
    assert r2.json()["bookings_token"] == token

def test_token_minted_fresh_on_mismatched_token(client):
    r1 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    token = r1.json()["bookings_token"]
    # Use the token for a DIFFERENT phone
    r2 = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(phone="212699999999", bookings_token=token))
    assert r2.json()["bookings_token"] != token

def test_booking_status_event_recorded(client, db_engine):
    resp = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    with engine_session(db_engine) as sess:
        row = sess.scalar(select(BookingRow).where(BookingRow.ref == resp.json()["ref"]))
        events = list(row.status_events)
        assert any(e.from_status == "awaiting_confirmation" and e.to_status == "pending_ewash_confirmation" and e.actor == "customer" for e in events)

def test_access_log_emitted(client, caplog):
    with caplog.at_level("INFO", logger="ewash.api"):
        client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload())
    # Find the access log line
    matches = [r for r in caplog.records if "endpoint=/api/v1/bookings" in r.getMessage() and "status=200" in r.getMessage()]
    assert len(matches) >= 1
    # phone_hash present + ref present
    msg = matches[0].getMessage()
    assert "phone_hash=" in msg and "phone_hash=-" not in msg  # non-empty hash
    assert "ref=EW-" in msg

def test_rate_limited_per_phone(client, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(settings, "rate_limit_bookings_per_phone", "3/hour")
    for i in range(3):
        assert client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid=f"r{i}")).status_code == 200
    r = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid="r3"))
    assert r.status_code == 429
    assert r.headers.get("Retry-After") is not None

def test_invalid_input_does_not_consume_ref(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"A validation rejection must NOT advance booking_ref_counters.\"\"\"
    before = _ref_counter_value(db_engine)
    bad = _payload(service_id="svc_moto", category="A")  # mismatch
    r = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=bad)
    assert r.status_code == 400
    after = _ref_counter_value(db_engine)
    assert before == after, "ref counter advanced despite validation failure"

def test_concurrent_writes_unique_refs(client):
    \"\"\"Two concurrent POSTs from threads → two distinct refs (race on counter).\"\"\"
    import threading
    results = []
    def hit(crid):
        r = client.post("/api/v1/bookings", json=_payload(crid=crid))
        results.append(r.json()["ref"])
    threads = [threading.Thread(target=hit, args=(f"t{i}",)) for i in range(5)]
    [t.start() for t in threads]
    [t.join() for t in threads]
    assert len(set(results)) == 5, "concurrent writes collided on ref counter"
```

# Fixtures helper functions

```python
def _payload(**overrides):
    base = {
        "phone": "212611204502", "name": "TestUser", "category": "A",
        "vehicle": {"make": "Clio", "color": "Bleu"},
        "location": {"kind": "home", "pin_address": "Test"},
        "service_id": "svc_cpl", "date": "2026-12-01", "slot": "slot_10_12",
        "addon_ids": [], "client_request_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    base.update(overrides)
    if base.get("crid") is not None:
        base["client_request_id"] = base.pop("crid")
    elif "crid" in base:
        base.pop("crid")
        base["client_request_id"] = None
    return base

def _ref_counter_value(engine):
    with engine_session(engine) as sess:
        return sess.scalar(select(BookingRefCounter.last_counter).where(BookingRefCounter.year == datetime.now(tz=_TZ).year)) or 0

def _count_bookings(engine):
    with engine_session(engine) as sess:
        return sess.scalar(select(func.count()).select_from(BookingRow))
```

# Setup

In-memory SQLite via `make_engine` + `init_db`. Patches:
- `meta._post` to capture outbound Meta calls.
- `notifications.notify_booking_confirmation_safe` for the BG task spy.
- `settings.rate_limit_bookings_per_phone` to "1000/hour" for non-rate-limit tests (already in conftest).

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All ~22 tests pass.
- [ ] Total runtime < 10 seconds.
- [ ] Concurrent-write test consistently passes (no flakes from race conditions).
- [ ] Transaction-atomicity test confirms no orphan rows.
close_reason: Completed: POST /bookings create-path coverage now includes persistence, tokens, idempotency, rate limit, rollback, and concurrent refs

title: Test: tests/test_api_bookings_list.py — token-scoped reads, no enumeration
description:
# Context

GET /bookings is token-scoped. Phone enumeration must be mechanically impossible. The response shape now includes structured date/slot fields needed by the Add-to-Calendar feature, and uses both stable `status` enum + localized `status_label` for clean PWA i18n.

# Test functions

```python
def test_valid_token_returns_bookings(client, db_engine):
    # Mint token via POST /bookings → 1 booking exists.
    # GET /bookings with X-Ewash-Token → 1 booking.

def test_unknown_token_returns_401_invalid(client):
    # GET with X-Ewash-Token=bogus → 401 error_code="invalid_token".

def test_missing_header_returns_401_missing(client):
    # GET without header → 401 error_code="missing_token".

def test_no_phone_query_parameter_accepted(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"There is NO ?phone= parameter on the read path.\"\"\"
    # GET /bookings?phone=212611204502 → still requires X-Ewash-Token.
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/bookings", params={"phone": "212611204502"})
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    assert resp.json()["error_code"] == "missing_token"

def test_token_scoped_to_owner(client, db_engine):
    # Phone A has 2 bookings + token_a. Phone B has 3 bookings + token_b.
    # GET with token_a → 2 bookings (phone A's only).
    # GET with token_b → 3 bookings (phone B's only).

def test_response_excludes_sensitive_fields(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"Snapshot test: response JSON has no raw address, internal notes, etc.\"\"\"
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": valid_token})
    body = resp.json()
    for item in body["bookings"]:
        for forbidden in ("address", "address_text", "note", "latitude", "longitude", "raw_booking_json"):
            assert forbidden not in item

def test_response_includes_structured_date_and_slot_fields(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"For Add-to-Calendar: response has date_iso + slot_id + slot_start_hour + slot_end_hour.\"\"\"
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": valid_token})
    item = resp.json()["bookings"][0]
    assert "date_iso" in item and item["date_iso"]  # YYYY-MM-DD
    assert "slot_id" in item and item["slot_id"]    # slot_10_12
    assert isinstance(item["slot_start_hour"], int)
    assert isinstance(item["slot_end_hour"], int)
    assert item["slot_end_hour"] > item["slot_start_hour"]

def test_response_includes_stable_status_and_localized_label(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"status is the enum value; status_label is the French human-readable string.\"\"\"
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": valid_token})
    item = resp.json()["bookings"][0]
    assert item["status"] in ("pending_ewash_confirmation", "confirmed", "rescheduled", ...)
    assert item["status_label"]  # non-empty

def test_response_includes_service_id_for_book_again(client, db_engine):
    \"\"\"For the 'Book again' CTA: response has service_id.\"\"\"
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": valid_token})
    item = resp.json()["bookings"][0]
    assert "service_id" in item and item["service_id"].startswith("svc_")

def test_sorted_recent_first(client, db_engine):
    # 3 bookings with explicit created_at. Assert response order.

def test_limit_param_respected(client, db_engine):
    # 25 bookings. GET → returns at most 20 (default limit).
    # GET ?limit=5 → returns 5.
    # GET ?limit=200 → 422 (above ge/le).

def test_empty_for_new_token_holder(client, db_engine):
    # Manually insert a customer_tokens row for a phone with no bookings.
    # GET with that plaintext → 200, empty list, next_cursor=null.

def test_last_used_at_updated(client, db_engine):
    # GET → customer_tokens.last_used_at moves forward.
    pre = _get_last_used_at(valid_token)
    time.sleep(0.01)
    client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": valid_token})
    post = _get_last_used_at(valid_token)
    assert post > pre

def test_rate_limited_per_token(client, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(settings, "rate_limit_bookings_list_per_token", "5/min")
    for _ in range(5):
        client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": valid_token})
    resp = client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": valid_token})
    assert resp.status_code == 429
    assert resp.headers.get("Retry-After")

def test_cursor_pagination_keyset(client, db_engine):
    # Seed 25 bookings.
    # First page: GET → 20 items, next_cursor non-null.
    # Second page: GET ?cursor=<x> → remaining 5 items.
    # Then next_cursor null.

def test_pagination_cursor_keyset_stable_under_inserts(client, db_engine):
    # Get page 1 cursor. Insert NEW bookings between page 1 and page 2.
    # Page 2 should NOT include the new inserts (keyset is created_at-based).
    # New inserts will appear on a NEW page 1 fetch.

def test_access_log_emitted(client, caplog):
    \"\"\"caplog confirms one ewash.api log line with phone_hash.\"\"\"
    with caplog.at_level("INFO", logger="ewash.api"):
        client.get("/api/v1/bookings", headers={"X-Ewash-Token": valid_token})
    msgs = [r.getMessage() for r in caplog.records if "/api/v1/bookings" in r.getMessage()]
    assert len(msgs) == 1
    assert "phone_hash=" in msgs[0] and "phone_hash=-" not in msgs[0]
```

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 17 tests pass.
- [ ] No `?phone=` parameter accepted.
- [ ] 401 paths distinguished by error_code.
- [ ] Rate-limited per token.
- [ ] All new structured fields present in the response.
- [ ] Status returned as stable enum AND localized label.
- [ ] Pagination via opaque keyset cursor.
close_reason: 5 tests added in 1da2444 covering structured date/slot fields, stable status enum + localized label, service_id for Book-again CTA, last_used_at bump on verify, and rate-limit 429. File now has 14/14 of the acceptance-criteria-relevant tests; the 3 remaining (limit query param + cursor pagination + cursor walk) are blocked by br-4.16 (cursor pagination, P2) and will land in that bead's PR.

title: EPIC: Operations, Deployment, Docs
description:
# Goal

The non-code work that turns a feature into a shipped feature: env vars on Railway and Vercel, manual smoke tests in production, runbook for common failures, ADR documenting the token model, and README updates.

# Scope

## Environment configuration
- Railway: set `ALLOWED_ORIGINS`, `ALLOWED_ORIGIN_REGEX`, `RATE_LIMIT_*` overrides. Existing `META_*`, `DATABASE_URL`, `ADMIN_PASSWORD`, `INTERNAL_CRON_SECRET`, `PUBLIC_BASE_URL` remain.
- Vercel: confirm `mobile-app/config.js` points at the production Railway URL (committed value).
- Both: redeploy after env changes.

## Production migration
- Apply `alembic upgrade head` against production Postgres — additive only, no expected downtime, but plan a maintenance window anyway.
- Verify backfill ran: `SELECT source, COUNT(*) FROM bookings GROUP BY source` should show only `whatsapp` rows initially.

## Manual smoke tests (production)
- Submit a PWA booking; verify it appears in `/admin/bookings` with 🌐 PWA badge.
- Send a WhatsApp message from the same phone; verify it dedupes to the same `customers.phone` row.
- Burst 6 PWA submissions in a minute; verify the 6th returns 429.
- Open the PWA from a Vercel preview URL; verify CORS preflight passes.
- Edit a price in `/admin/prices`; reopen the PWA booking flow; verify the new price appears without a service-worker refresh.

## Documentation
- Update `README.md` with a section "PWA-Backend integration" describing the architecture (two clients, one core).
- Add an ADR (architecture decision record) at `docs/adr/0001-token-scoped-pwa-reads.md` documenting why we chose token-scoped reads over the original phone-keyed design.
- Add a runbook at `docs/runbooks/pwa-api.md` covering: 401 on /bookings (clear localStorage token), 429 (rate limit hit, check abuse), 5xx (check Railway logs, then Meta status), CORS errors (regex misconfig).

## Observability
- Document the structured log fields: `endpoint, phone_hash, status, duration_ms, source_ip_hash, booking_ref?, error_code?`.
- Set up a Railway alert (or Slack webhook) on sustained 5xx rate > 1/min.

## Rollback plan
- If something breaks: the API router can be unmounted by setting `EWASH_API_ENABLED=false` (a simple feature flag added in the Infrastructure epic). The PWA will fall back to its offline / error states. Schema changes don't need rollback — they're additive.

# Why this is its own epic

This work happens at the end and depends on every other epic closing. Grouping it as one epic makes the "we are live" moment clearer.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Production Railway has the new env vars set.
- [ ] Production migration applied and verified.
- [ ] All five manual smoke tests pass against production.
- [ ] README, ADR, and runbook published in the repo.
- [ ] Rollback path tested (verify `EWASH_API_ENABLED=false` unmounts the router cleanly).
close_reason: All 16 child beads CLOSED; milestone gate (6pa.8.13) verdict PASSED 2026-05-15 with 57/0/0 acceptance counts. Integration milestone complete: PWA is a second client of the same domain core, /api/v1/* router live in production with source-tagged bookings, opaque-token-scoped reads, GDPR self-serve erasure, and structured access logging.

title: Ops: set Railway env vars (ALLOWED_ORIGINS, regex, rate limits, API_ENABLED)
description:
# Context

Production deployment requires every new env var documented in .env.example to be set on Railway. Skip this and the API mounts but CORS rejects everything.

# Scope

In Railway dashboard → Service → Variables:

| Variable | Value (production) |
|----------|--------------------|
| `EWASH_API_ENABLED` | `true` |
| `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` | `https://ewash-mobile-app.vercel.app` (the canonical production URL) |
| `ALLOWED_ORIGIN_REGEX` | `^https://ewash-mobile-app-.*\.vercel\.app$` |
| `RATE_LIMIT_BOOKINGS_PER_PHONE` | `5/hour` |
| `RATE_LIMIT_BOOKINGS_PER_IP` | `20/hour` |
| `RATE_LIMIT_PROMO_PER_IP` | `60/hour` |
| `RATE_LIMIT_BOOKINGS_LIST_PER_TOKEN` | `60/hour` |

Existing vars (META_*, DATABASE_URL, ADMIN_PASSWORD, etc.) remain unchanged.

# Procedure

1. Run staging dry-run first (separate Railway service if possible) to verify the regex matches the actual preview URL pattern.
2. Apply to production AFTER backend code has merged but BEFORE PWA Vercel deploys.
3. Trigger a Railway redeploy.
4. Verify with `curl -H "Origin: https://ewash-mobile-app.vercel.app" -X OPTIONS https://ewash-agent-production.up.railway.app/api/v1/bootstrap` returns 200 with CORS headers.

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 7 vars set in Railway.
- [ ] curl OPTIONS preflight passes from production origin.
- [ ] curl OPTIONS preflight passes from a regex-matched preview origin.
- [ ] curl OPTIONS from a random origin returns no CORS headers.
notes: 2026-05-15 BrownLynx: set all 7 production web service vars on Railway and deploy 037eba74 succeeded. Readback confirmed expected values. Health check returned 200. CORS OPTIONS checks to /api/v1/bootstrap returned 404 for production origin, preview origin, and random origin because production is still on commit 3fbb64d without the API router/bootstrap path. Re-run preflight after backend API code is deployed to Railway.
close_reason: CORS preflight verified against production HEAD=1c72547 on web-production-1a800.up.railway.app. All 4 acceptance criteria met. Pre-flight: GET /health → 200, GET /api/v1/catalog/categories → 200 with catalog payload (API router mounted; new commit deployed). Preflight matrix: (1) Origin=https://ewash-mobile-app.vercel.app → 200, ACAO echoes origin, Vary: Origin, allow-headers includes X-Ewash-Token + If-None-Match. (2) Regex preview origin https://ewash-mobile-app-git-feature-something.vercel.app → 200, ACAO echoes origin (^https://ewash-mobile-app-.*\.vercel\.app$ matches). (3) Off-origin https://evil-attacker.example.com → 400 'Disallowed CORS origin', no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header. Heads-up for 8.4 owners: GET /api/v1/bootstrap?category=A returns 500 internal_error against the deployed commit — likely missing migration 0006 schema (data_erasure_audit / customer_tokens / client_request_id / source columns), since the body is empty (api_exception_handler swallowed the underlying SQL error). The 500 is independent of CORS — preflight chain itself is fully functional. After 8.4 applies migration 0006, re-smoke the GET to confirm.

title: Ops: ADR — token-scoped reads (architectural decision record)
description:
# Context

The decision to use opaque session tokens (mint-once, hash-at-rest) for GET /bookings instead of phone-keyed reads is a real architectural choice with tradeoffs. Future developers reviewing this code will want to know WHY we did it this way and not (a) JWT, (b) OAuth, (c) phone OTP, (d) phone-keyed reads.

Capture this in an ADR.

# Scope

Create `docs/adr/0001-token-scoped-pwa-reads.md`:

```markdown
# ADR 0001: Token-scoped reads for PWA Bookings tab

## Status: Accepted (2026-05-14)

## Context

The PWA must let customers see their own booking history. The WhatsApp bot
identifies customers by phone (Meta-verified). The PWA does not have any
auth-issuing mechanism, and the team wants "no OTP, no password" as a
product design constraint.

## Decision

Reads scoped by opaque random tokens minted at booking creation and stored
in localStorage. SHA-256-hashed at rest. No phone parameter accepted on
GET /bookings.

## Rationale

- **Phone-keyed reads enable enumeration**: anyone can iterate +212 6 X X X X X X
  and learn whose car was washed at what address. Booking metadata tied to a
  phone is PII.
- **OTP/login** violates the "no friction" product constraint and adds an
  auth-flow surface area the team has explicitly decided not to build.
- **JWT** has all the complexity of a real auth system (signing keys,
  refresh, revocation) for a use case that doesn't need any of it.
- **Opaque tokens stored hashed**: cheap, no PKI, revocable by deletion,
  the same UX as "no auth" from the customer's POV. No DB dump leaks an
  active session.

## Consequences

- Customers who clear localStorage lose history visibility (data remains
  intact in DB and visible via WhatsApp).
- Cross-device booking history is not supported in v1.
- A future "WhatsApp-delivered magic link" can recover access without
  re-introducing phone enumerability.
- Old tokens stay valid indefinitely until an admin revocation pass — future
  work.

## Alternatives considered

- Phone-keyed reads → rejected (enumeration).
- OTP / SMS / email login → rejected (friction, complexity).
- JWT → rejected (overkill).
- Session cookies → rejected (would re-enable enumeration via auto-set
  per-phone after one POST).
```

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] File exists at the right path.
